TextENGNew: ``......Sigh.@k@vS14/06/210600569. Kei-chan, you've come up with something outrageous~.``

That was Shion's first reaction after reading through the plan I'd written up. Whether she was impressed or exasperated was hard to tell, but at the very least she seemed surprised, and that gave me a small sense of satisfaction.

``When I heard a bit about it over the phone last night, I honestly thought you were talking in your sleep and just let it go.@k@vS14/06/210600571. I never imagined you were serious.@k@vS14/06/210600572. ...And your handwriting is terrible, by the way.``
``That's beside the point.@k@vS14/01/210101363. And what do you mean, talking in my sleep?@k@vS14/01/210101364. What kind of person do you think I am?``
``Do you really want to know?``
``No, I don't.``

If I actually heard her answer, I'd probably have to hole up somewhere and question the meaning of my entire existence. Proving my point, Shion puffed her cheeks and let out an annoyed ``Tch~`` at having lost the chance to tease me. ...This girl.

Meanwhile, next to Shion, the store manager Yoshiroh-san was reading through my plan with a serious expression. Since this shop is run by a Sonozaki family member, he's apparently Mion and Shion's uncle.
``Still... this is going to be quite a challenge, you know?@k@vS14/00/yosiro10002. Even if it's just a small festival stall, are you sure you can pull it off?``
``Yes.@k@vS14/01/210101367. I've already asked around and consulted people in the neighborhood association who know about this stuff.``

I puffed out my chest as I said that. In reality, the only reason a kid like me could get anyone to listen was thanks to Mion acting as an intermediary, but I'd done my research thoroughly enough to feel reasonably confident.
Following the bargain auction, this was the other main attraction I'd proposed for the Watanagashi festival.
Namely... an ``Angel Mort festival booth``!!
changeReason: Old translates 出店 (festival booth/stall) as 'branch store,' a permanent retail concept; new correctly says 'festival booth,' matching the temporary setup described in context.

TextENGNew: In other words, alongside the usual yakisoba, candy apple, and shooting gallery stalls, we'd set up an Angel Mort booth that would deliberately stand out as something different.
``Kei-chan, do you realize?@k@vS14/06/210600575. Festival stalls are clearly all traditional Japanese fare.``
``If we show up among all that in these flashy outfits, the elderly folks are going to keel over with nosebleeds, and I take no responsibility for that, okay?``
Shion said that while tugging at the chest of her uniform to show it off.

...No, having a lot of skin showing doesn't make something 'flashy,' and besides, who even uses the word 'flashy' like that anymore? Were you born in the Taisho era? ...I wanted to quip, but held my tongue since the store manager was right there.

``Ahahahaha, the older folks might actually love it, you know?@k@vS14/00/yosiro10004. After all, the only places in this village where you'd see young girls in outfits like these are the school or enthusiast shops like ours~!``
``...Please don't just casually say things like that.``
``Since it's a festival, you can just wear a happi coat over the uniform.``

``That way, it doubles as an excuse for the hard-headed adults who'd complain about it being improper.@k@vS14/01/210101370. And for those types, you can tell them 'if you want to see the rest, come visit our shop!' It's advertising, too.``
``Wow, that's sneaky...``
``At the very least, call me a strategist. And besides--``

This was something I'd been thinking about ever since the engagement uproar with Mion... or rather, my class rep inauguration party, when I watched the younger students eagerly stuffing their faces with Angel Mort cake.
Surprisingly, most of the kids--aside from a few brave souls like Daiki, Suguru, and Kameda-kun--either didn't know about Angel Mort's cakes or had barely ever tried them.
There was one simple reason for that. ...Ironically, it was Angel Mort's biggest selling point: the uniforms.

Because of those uniforms, the customer base inevitably skewed toward 'hardcore' male regulars, creating an atmosphere that drove away female customers--especially housewives, the ones who actually hold the purse strings.
As a result, ordinary kids had no idea how good this shop's cakes really were.
``Well, that's true... I'm confident they'd be hooked after just one visit, but bringing the whole family in might be a bit of a high hurdle.``

``A hurdle? More like the high jump... no, the pole vault.@k@vS14/01/210101373. That's exactly why I think this is the perfect opportunity to get people to really know Angel Mort... What do you say?``

``Hmm, let me think~.@k@vS14/00/yosiro10006. In terms of revenue, we're doing fine as we are, but...@k@vS14/00/yosiro10007. as a shop that sells sweets, we definitely want kids and 'young women' eating here too.``
``...You put a lot of emphasis on that last part, didn't you.``

``Wah ha ha ha!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10009. Well, even though I started this shop as a hobby, I've already achieved my dream of working surrounded by young gals!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10010. Naturally, my next goal is to get close to adult women too! That's just human nature!!``
What kind of human nature is that...? Why are all the men around me so honest about their desires?
``Sigh... Very well then.@k@vS14/06/210600582. I can't say I'm not interested, either.``
``With that settled, here you go.@k@vS14/06/210600584. For reference, this is an estimate of the product costs and expenses.``

Shion spread out a handwritten product list compiled on report paper across the table. ...For someone who claimed I was talking in my sleep, she sure had everything prepared. How dishonest of her... That's Mion's sister for you.
``I mean... cakes have an incredible markup.@k@vS14/01/210101375. You sell them at that price with material costs this low?``
changeReason: Old says 'that's awfully expensive for some cakes' (suggesting high material costs); new correctly conveys the JPN meaning that the markup is incredible--materials are cheap yet selling price is high (すごい原価 + こんな材料費であれだけの価格).

TextENGNew: ``What are you implying? How rude, making it sound like some rip-off bar.@k@vS14/06/210600586. We have higher labor costs, costume maintenance, and all sorts of other expenses that normal shops don't.@k@vS14/06/210600587. We've actually put a lot of effort into keeping the prices reasonable, you know?``
``On top of that, there are the cleaning costs after you and your friends trashed the place, and the repair costs for the restroom you broke...``
``...Sorry about that.@k@vS14/01/210101377. I went overboard that time.@k@vS14/01/210101378. I really am sorry.``
Seriously, she's so persistent... They call it having a 'sticky' personality, and that fits her to a T.
``Wahahahaha!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10012. Oh, I've heard all about that legendary tale~?@k@vS14/00/yosiro10013. Shion-chan told me about it with the biggest grin on her face~!``
``H-hey, Uncle!@k@vS14/06/210600590. Don't say unnecessary things!``
Shion's face turned red as she jabbed her elbow into the store manager's side. ...Well, I did cause trouble, but if Shion found it amusing enough to tell people about it, I suppose that's fine.

``...Oh, but Kei-chan, did you think about this?@k@vS14/06/210600592. Festival food is fundamentally meant to be eaten while walking around.@k@vS14/06/210600593. Forks and plates are hard to use on the go, and even disposable ones would drive up costs, right?``
``But can't you just do it the same way as yakisoba or takoyaki?@k@vS14/01/210101380. Disposable chopsticks and cheap containers...``
``No, no, just imagine it, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/06/210600595. Do you think eating cake with chopsticks would look appetizing to either the person eating or anyone watching?``
That's true... it would look completely out of place, and the image was frankly depressing. But as Shion pointed out, plastic forks and paper plates would be too expensive...
``...Oh, what about wrapping the cake in crepe batter?@k@vS14/00/waitA10002. Your hands won't get dirty, and since you eat the wrapper with the cake, there's hardly any garbage.``
Shion and I looked up toward the voice. Several waitresses had gathered near our table at some point without us noticing.
``Sorry for butting in.@k@vS14/00/waitB10002. ...But since we'll be participating too, could we help brainstorm ideas?``
``Oh, no, that would be a huge help, but...@k@vS14/06/210600597. Don't you all have work to do?``
You're one to talk.
``We're on our break right now.@k@vS14/00/waitC10002. If possible, we'd love to discuss what we can offer after closing time...@k@vS14/00/waitC10003. How about it, boss?``
``You're really okay with that?@k@vS14/00/yosiro10015. Watanagashi is only three days away. You're sure you want to help on such short notice?``
``Of course!@k@vS14/00/waitA10004. It sounds like fun--like a school cultural festival!``
``Besides, we're doing it for Sonozaki-san's sweetheart, after all~.``
The waitresses exchanged smiles and nods. Shion instantly turned so red it looked like steam was about to pour off her. ...Why?
``Wh-wh-wh-whose sweetheart?!@k@vS14/06/210600599. You're thinking of my sister!@k@vS14/06/210600600. The tacky Sonozaki!@k@vS14/06/210600601. I'm the pretty Sonozaki!!``
``Wahahahaha~!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10017. Playing both Sonozaki sisters, Maebara-kun? You're a bold man!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10018. By all means, go fight Akane-san and claim total victory!!``
``Gwah?!``

The store manager burst into hearty laughter and slapped my back with tremendous force. I choked from the impact, which nearly knocked the wind out of me, while the waitresses worked themselves into a frenzy around us.
``All right!@k@vS14/00/yosiro10020. Since we're all participating, let's make it so exciting that when people think of Watanagashi, they think of Angel Mort!``
``If sales hit a new festival record, I'll throw in a special bonus!!``
``And whoever makes the most sales gets invited to dinner with yours truly!!``
````No thank you.````
``They're tough, aren't they?@k@vS14/06/210600603. Our waitresses, I mean.``
``...That's not tough. That's brazen.``
These waitresses had pretty faces but they were absolutely merciless.
``Oh right, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210101386. Here, I'm returning this.``
After our discussion wrapped up and the store manager left for a neighborhood meeting, I pulled Takano's scrapbook out of my bag and set it on the table in front of Shion.
``Oh, you've read it already?@k@vS14/06/210600605. ...So, what did you think?``
``What do I think...@k@vS14/01/210101388. It's absurd, is what it is. A total crackpot book.``
Honestly, I didn't feel like I gained much from reading it. Most of the article clippings and notes inside were built on speculation and groundless fabrications.

``Oyashiro-sama is an alien?@k@vS14/01/210101390. ...And controlling the villagers by spreading a virus?@k@vS14/01/210101391. The Furude family is the object of worship, supported by the Kimiyoshi and Sonozaki families, while those who lack faith are cursed?``
``...How do I put it... It's all just wild conjecture, and none of it made any sense.``

``I feel the same way.@k@vS14/06/210600607. I skimmed through it too, but it's all written from such an irresponsible position, with the author just saying whatever she pleased, that it became ridiculous by the halfway point.``
``Though the parts about human experimentation and using live humans as test subjects for virus research were kind of entertaining, in an occult-magazine sort of way.``
``...Shion, what do you think?@k@vS14/01/210101394. About the curse.``
``What... do you mean?@k@vS14/06/210600610. What specifically about the curse?``
I shared with Shion the theory about the curse that I'd explained to Rika-chan a few days ago. I wanted to hear what she thought about the incident with the Furude couple two years ago.
``...Hmm~, to get straight to the point...@k@vS14/06/210600612. I'd say there's absolutely zero chance the Sonozaki family was involved.``
Shion stated that without a hint of hesitation. Her swift, decisive answer actually caught me off guard.
``First point:@k@vS14/06/210600614. If the goal is to strengthen religious awe, the Sonozaki family would have no reason to harm the Furude family.``

``Second point:@k@vS14/06/210600616. Given that the Sonozaki family already held power in this village five years before the dam project, it's unthinkable they'd need to conspire to usurp the Furude family.``

``And the third point: if they harmed the Furude family, the resulting police investigation could uncover the Sonozaki family's own dark secrets.@k@vS14/06/210600618. ...Based on these three points, the Sonozaki family simply had no reason to act against the Furude family.``
'I learned all this from talking with Sis,' she added with a shrug.
``But in reality, the Sonozaki family's influence has grown thanks to the fear surrounding the curse, hasn't it?@k@vS14/01/210101396. So can you really say there was no benefit at all?``

``That's true.@k@vS14/06/210600620. ...But at the same time, it's made it harder for the Sonozaki family to act freely.@k@vS14/06/210600621. Recently, the Sonozaki family has... This is a secret, okay?@k@vS14/06/210600622. According to Sis, they're planning to transform the entire village in the near future by taking advantage of a highway construction project nearby.``
``But... thanks to the curse hanging over everything, they can't make that public.@k@vS14/06/210600624. If any kind of incident were to occur, people would immediately blame it on the curse.``
``In the worst case, the Sonozaki family could end up being treated like a second Houjou family.``
I see... Persecuted by the villagers as traitors who brought disaster to the village. In a feudalistic society like this, even a small rumor or bit of gossip could easily upend someone's entire life.

``So in other words... whoever created this curse system doesn't want the environment of Hinamizawa to change.@k@vS14/01/210101398. They're unconsciously binding the villagers' behavior... Is that what you're saying?``
``That's right.@k@vS14/06/210600627. Takano-san's theories are outlandish, but if these serial incidents aren't mere coincidence, whatever's behind them is going to be far from simple to unravel.``

...The more I listened to Shion, the more it seemed like this conspiracy wasn't something personal to Miyo Takano. If that was the case, did that mean this 'Tokyo' organization behind the scenes had some larger scheme at play...?
``By the way, there's something about Rika-chama that's been bothering me-- Oh.``
Shion had started to lean forward as if she'd remembered something, then glanced to the side and stopped herself.
...After confirming that a customer who had just entered was walking past our table, she lowered her voice and continued.
``It's about Satoko and Rika-chama.@k@vS14/06/210600630. Kei-chan, did you know they've apparently been going to the Irie Clinic about once a week?``
``Huh? No, this is the first I've heard of it.@k@vS14/01/210101401. Are they not feeling well?``

``Hmm~, I heard from Satoko that...@k@vS14/06/210600632. they're apparently working part-time as test subjects for some new medication.@k@vS14/06/210600633. The Coach has been collecting data on drug administration each time, and it's been going on for nearly three years now.``
``She said that's how they've been covering their living expenses.``
Testing experimental drugs... to cover living expenses? I had always assumed they were getting by with support from Mayor Kimiyoshi.
Setting that aside... a new drug, huh.
Rika-chan had told me she was cooperating with the Coach's research on Hinamizawa Syndrome, but this was the first I'd heard about Satoko being involved.
...And despite viewing Miyo Takano as an enemy, why was she cooperating with that research...?
``...Have you told anyone else about this?``
``Huh? No, not yet.@k@vS14/06/210600636. You're the first, Kei-chan.``
``Then keep it between us for now.@k@vS14/01/210101404. Don't tell Mion either.``

``I don't mind, but...@k@vS14/06/210600638. are you sure that's wise, dear brother-in-law?@k@vS14/06/210600639. Starting to share secrets with your beloved wife's little sister... things could get complicated, you know.@k@vS14/06/210600640. Heh heh heh!``
``Why does it always turn into that?!``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS14/06/210600642. I'm just kidding, sheesh--...?``
Just then, the senior waitress who had suggested the crepe idea earlier walked over to our table from the back.
``Oh, sorry, senpai.@k@vS14/06/210600644. I'll get back to work in a moment.``
``No, that's not it.@k@vS14/00/waitA10008. There's a phone call for you, Sonozaki-san. From someone named Kasai-san.``
``...From Kasai?``
The moment she heard the name, Shion tilted her head with a dubious look. Then, excusing herself with a quick 'Just a moment,' she hurried off toward the back.
``...It's about time to head out.``
I had a Watanagashi planning meeting at the assembly hall on the Furude Shrine grounds today. Now that we'd settled things about the food booth, maybe I'd pay up and leave once Shion got back...
``K-Kei-chan! This is bad!!``
Just then, Shion came rushing back with her face completely drained of color. Sensing that something was terribly wrong, a bad feeling washed over me and I stood up, bracing myself.
``What happened, Shion?!``
``R-Rina Mamiya was...!``
Perhaps from sheer shock, Shion took two deep breaths to steady her tangled words and emotions. Then, her flushed face turning pale in an instant, she forced the words out.
``Rina Mamiya... has been murdered.@k@vS14/06/210600649. They just found the body...!``
For a moment, the shock hit me like a wave and I lost my voice. ...After everything we did to keep her alive and stop Teppei from coming to Hinamizawa...!
``Don't tell me...?``

``Let me say upfront--the Sonozakis aren't involved.@k@vS14/06/210600651. Kasai did threaten her, but his plan was to transfer her to another establishment when the time was right. They didn't lay a hand on her.``
Anticipating my suspicions, Shion spoke in a hushed tone to head me off. Then who on earth...?!
``Forget about that, Kei-chan--we need to worry about Satoko!@k@vS14/06/210600653. Is Rika-chama at home?!``
``No, there's a Watanagashi planning meeting today, so she should be at the assembly hall.@k@vS14/01/210101411. What's the phone number?``
``I know it! Right away!``
The moment she said that, Shion bolted back toward the phone. There were still quite a few customers in the restaurant, but she couldn't afford to worry about that.
Satoko... Don't tell me she's already been taken...
``...Pardon me.``
Just then... a man stepped out of the takeout line near the entrance and walked over to me.
He raised a hand apologetically as I eyed him with suspicion, then spoke in a questioning tone.
``Just now... did you mention someone named Rika-chan...?``
I was desperate.
I thought of nothing else, cared about nothing else... I just kept searching for her.
``Satoko, Satoko...!``
I shouted desperately.
Passersby turned to look, wondering what was going on. The usual elderly people approached to ask what was wrong.
But nothing registered. I didn't have the composure to talk to any of them. Their bewildered stares felt like nothing but an annoyance right now.
``Please... Satoko, if you can hear me, please answer!``
My throat hurt.
My knees throbbed with pain from where I'd scraped them when I tripped earlier.
But more than any of that, my heart ached.
Please.
Please, please be safe.
Even if the timing was the worst possible, even if I arrived at the last possible moment--
I prayed with everything I had. I didn't stop to think whether it was to God or anyone else--I just kept praying.
Even in this world, I'd had that premonition.
It was Satoko's turn to run errands today, but on this day of all days, I felt an inexplicable reluctance to let her go.
I couldn't put it into words. But as I watched Satoko pick up the shopping basket and get ready to leave, a chilling wave of anxiety rose from deep within my chest.
This feeling... I'd experienced something like it in a previous world. When Teppei came back and took Satoko away... Yes, it was exactly like this.

Working with Keiichi and the others, I'd devised the best possible plan to keep Rina alive. Thanks to that, Rina's survival was confirmed even past the day she was supposed to die, and past the day her body would have been discovered.
Only three days until Watanagashi. Based on all previous iterations, things should have been safe by this point... And yet, the premonition I hated most had come--of all times, now.
On top of that, Satoko had a slight fever today, possibly a summer cold. ...So I spoke up, intending to let her rest.
``Satoko... I'll take your turn for errands today.@k@vS14/05/210500760. You can stay home and rest.``
``I'm fine, Rika.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1005. My headache is already going away, and I think a short walk outside will help me feel better.``
``You shouldn't push yourself.@k@vS14/05/210500762. ...I'll go shopping after the meeting, so please just stay home and rest.``
``Oh, you're such a worrywart, Rika!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1007. But you've been so busy lately with meetings and rehearsals every day. I can't keep putting all this burden on you.``
``It's fine.@k@vS14/05/210500764. You really don't need to worry about it, so...``

``Besides, I've finally discovered how to tell broccoli and cauliflower apart!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1009. This time I'll buy the right one so you won't have to laugh at me, Rika! Look forward to it!``
``Oh ho ho ho ho!``
``Satoko--``
Ignoring my protests, Satoko grabbed the shopping basket, left the room, and clattered down the stairs.
...Still worried, I looked down from the window and saw Satoko straddling her bicycle, waving up at me.

Her mischievous innocence was so endearing that I couldn't help but let out a little laugh as I waved back. And as I watched her pedal away, the uneasy feeling that had been nagging at my heart seemed to fade, if only slightly.
Maybe I was just overthinking it. ...Between trying to figure out how to explain Takano to Tomitake and Irie, and everything else going on, my nerves must have been on edge.
By this point in time, Satoko had always been safe. All I needed to do was keep an eye on Takano and her group.
Telling myself that, I decided to get changed and head out to the Watanagashi meeting...
Fool, fool, fool, fool, fool, fool, fool--you absolute fool!!
Huge beads of sweat rolled down my forehead.
I had been running nonstop and was completely out of breath, the acrid taste of blood rising in the back of my throat.
As I hunched over clutching my knees, trying to catch my breath, my legs trembled uncontrollably from the hips down, drained of all strength.
...It happened during the meeting, when I got up to use the restroom and was on my way back.
I overheard a group of elderly people huddled in a corner, clasping their hands and murmuring 'heaven help us' as they talked.
``...A woman's body?``
``That's what they say... apparently she was an 'employee' from the entertainment district in Okinomiya.@k@vS14/00/eldB10003. They found her in the drainage ditch behind some apartments...``
``It hadn't been long since she died, but...@k@vS14/00/eldC10003. they say she suffocated with mud clogged in her throat.``
``They're saying she jumped in herself?@k@vS14/00/eldD10002. What on earth could she have been thinking...?``
When I heard that.
Every drop of blood in my body froze in an instant, and I became unable to move, as if even my breathing had stopped.
Something dark churned and swelled inside my chest. My legs gave out beneath me and I sank to the ground, as though struck by vertigo.
``R-Rika-chama?@k@vS14/00/eldB10005. Are you all right?!``
``Why would you talk about such things here?!@k@vS14/00/eldA10004. Go discuss it outside, you fools!``
``Don't worry, Rika-chama.@k@vS14/00/eldC10005. The youngsters just heard some silly rumor, that's all.``
The elders must have thought I was shocked from hearing such a gruesome story. They rushed over in a panic, each trying to comfort and cheer me up.
...But their words didn't reach me. My heart was filled with nothing but shock... and despair.
Why?
Why has it happened again?
Was I betrayed, despite everything I told Kasai and Shion?
Or... is this fate itself declaring that it cannot be changed?!
``......That's right--Satoko!``
I snapped back to my senses, mustered all my strength, and rushed out of the room.
Then I spotted the self-defense tool sitting on the shoe rack by the entrance, shoved it into my pocket, roughly crammed on my shoes, and flung open the front door.
``R-Rika-chama?!``
I heard someone calling after me from behind. But I couldn't afford to waste even a second, so I left the assembly hall without looking back.
``Haah, haah, haah, haah... haah...!``
I was wheezing, struggling for breath, my lungs and windpipe constricting painfully.
Both sides of my abdomen ached as if clamped in a vise. I no longer had the strength to wipe away the sweat pouring from my body, and my thin clothes clung to me, drenched and uncomfortable.
Is it happening again?
Am I going to fail to find her, and taste that same despair all over again?
I can't take it anymore.
I've had enough.
What satisfaction is there in plunging Satoko and me into despair over and over?
She must have been taken.
The reason I can't find her even after searching this desperately is because Teppei has already taken her away.
Why didn't I save her?
Why didn't anyone stop him?
I've thought this in every previous world... Why is everyone in Hinamizawa so heartlessly cold toward Satoko Houjou...?!
``Satoko, Satoko, Satoko, Sato-- Ah?!``
My exhausted legs tangled and I fell ungracefully to the ground. A housewife passing nearby started to approach and offer a hand, but I raised my head and glared back at her.
The woman stared at me with a bewildered expression, a look of confused pity on her face.
Every single one of them, watching me from a distance with those fake, sympathetic eyes, pretending to care like hypocrites.
...If you have time for that, then why won't you tell me about Satoko? Can't you see how desperately I've been searching, how ragged I've become? Can't you understand how serious this is?!
This area isn't that big--there's no way nobody saw Satoko and Teppei!
Please...! Anyone, I don't care who--tell me! Tell me you saw Satoko!
If she's safe, that's all that matters. If someone protected her from Teppei, I'll spend my whole life repaying them!
Even if that's not the case, if anyone knows anything at all about Satoko, say something! Please...!!
``...Rika-chama...?``

I looked up. It was the lady from the vegetable shop. She always gave me special treatment... but never to Satoko when she came alone. I never liked this woman, who so blatantly changed her behavior depending on whom she was dealing with.
And then... the shopkeeper lowered her eyes with a pained expression and spoke quietly.
``...It would be better if you didn't get involved with that Teppei Houjou anymore...``
``You saw Satoko, didn't you?!``
I sprang to my feet and pressed close to her. When I saw her awkwardly avert her eyes, my conviction only grew stronger.
``Teppei came here, didn't he?@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1029. Why?!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1030. Why didn't you stop him?!``
``...I'm so sorry...@k@vS14/00/yaoya10004. I'm truly sorry...``
``If you feel sorry enough to apologize--!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1032. You could have at least hidden her, you could have-- ...What?!``
That's when I noticed.
The vegetable shop behind the woman... was a wreck.
The front display shelves had been violently overturned, with several tomatoes crushed, their juice staining the pavement red.
The noticeably sparse selection of vegetables was probably because the rest had been damaged beyond saving.
...In the corner of the shop, I could see soiled cabbages piled into cardboard boxes.
And then.
Looking more closely, the woman's legs were covered in blue bruises, as if she'd been kicked. ...It took me only a moment to realize who had done this.

I looked around. The old man from the dried goods shop had torn clothes. The young man from the liquor store was holding his cheek. Everyone... they had all fought. They had stood up to protect Satoko...
And yet here I was, carried away by my emotions, shouting, glaring, doubting them all...!
``...I'm so sorry...``
I bowed deeply, from the very bottom of my heart. ...I had been on the verge of becoming the same kind of wretched person as Teppei.
No, it was worse than that.
I was the one who had lectured Keiichi and the others about not doubting people, about not harboring murderous intent... and yet here I was, acting like this...
``...Rika-chama.``
``You honor us too much, Rika-chama...``
They approached me, looking apologetic, trying to comfort me. ...But I flashed them a bright smile, held them back, then turned on my heel and started walking.
My resolve... was set.
......I never wanted to come here again.
Satoko's real home--the Houjou house.
Since Satoko and I started living together last year, this house had been empty.
At first, Satoko would occasionally come back to pick up seasonal clothes and daily necessities. ...But once she'd moved most of her things over, she stopped coming back entirely.
...For Satoko too, this house was nothing but a painful place that reminded her of her wretched life with her aunt and uncle, and of Satoshi. She must have been avoiding it.
But... my reason for hating this house is a little different.
...It's because whenever this house becomes relevant, I know what kind of world I'm in.
But.
This time, I came here with different feelings than in the previous worlds.
My purpose is one thing, and one thing only.
The second-floor window of the Houjou house was open.
Perhaps the other windows had been opened for ventilation too--soot-colored curtains were fluttering from inside.
Exactly the same as the previous world.
And yet this time... my knees weren't trembling at all.
An unfamiliar motorcycle was parked beside the house, with futons and laundry hung out to dry.
...Unmistakable signs of someone living here.
Proof that someone was residing in the Houjou house.
As for who that someone was... I didn't even need to ask.
It seemed Satoko had come out to bring in the drying futon and noticed me...
Satoko kept her head hung low, her expression a forced smile barely concealing her sadness... It was painful just to look at.
And on her face were painful marks... Don't look away. Look carefully, Rika.
``Satoko... you shouldn't be in a place like this. Let's go home.``
I reached for Satoko's hand. ...But her arms hung limply at her sides as she bowed her head even further...
``Hey... Rika...@k@vS14/04/210400252. I'm... going to be living in this house from now on...``
A refrain. Lines repeated from past worlds. Words I never wanted to hear again came spilling from Satoko's mouth.
``My... my uncle came back...@k@vS14/04/210400254. So, I'll be living with him now... in this house... That's how it is...``
``...Is that really how you feel, Satoko?@k@vS14/05/210500780_781_02. Is living with Teppei what you truly want...?``

``But... there's nothing I can do about it...@k@vS14/04/210400254_255_02. Even if I go back to your house, Rika... he'd just come and drag me back...@k@vS14/04/210400254_255_03. I'd only cause trouble for you, Rika...``
A sad smile of utter resignation.
...Standing before me was the same Satoko as a year ago... the Satoko of 1982. The same Satoko I had seen in the previous world.
Like a needle tracing the grooves of a record, the same scenes, the same conversations repeated themselves.
The cheerful, lively Satoko I'd been living with until just yesterday was already gone...
From here on... Satoko would suffer terribly at her uncle's hands. But eventually, Keiichi's efforts would rescue her from this crisis.

But... last time, this happened much earlier than Watanagashi. So even though it took time to save her, she was able to enjoy the festival with everyone. This time, Watanagashi is the day after tomorrow.
Which means... Satoko won't be able to take part in this year's Watanagashi...?
...Satoko, romping around energetically with a cheerful smile on her face.
That happy scene.
That moment of joy.
It warped into a murky gray, bleeding and blurring... sinking into the darkness. As I helplessly watched it vanish, a festering pain welled up inside my chest.
``Satokooo!!@k@vS14/21/212000002. Quit slacking off!!@k@vS14/21/212000003. Where'd ya go?!``
That vile, angry voice reached my ears.
Satoko flinched, her shoulders trembling, and stared down into empty space.
``...Haaah?@k@vS14/21/212000005. Who the hell are you?``
...That man appeared behind Satoko, looming over her.
``I-it's nothing.@k@vS14/04/210400256. A friend just came to visit, that's all...``
``Like I give a damn!!@k@vS14/21/212000007. Get in there and scrub the bathtub!@k@vS14/21/212000008. I wanna take a bath, ya hear me?!``
Satoko trembled violently and began hurrying back into the house, like a frightened little animal scurrying into a corner.
The misery of her retreating figure tore at my heart, and I--
``...Wait, Satoko.``
Satoko stopped just as she was about to enter the house and turned around at my call.

``Were you two going to play?@k@vS14/21/212000010. Sorry 'bout that, but Satoko here's a bit busy cleanin' the house.@k@vS14/21/212000011. I'm her uncle.@k@vS14/21/212000012. Been away on a loooong business trip.@k@vS14/21/212000013. Finally made it back to Hinamizawa, y'see.``
...What is he even saying?
Going out of his way to feed a pack of lies to a child like me--what does he hope to accomplish?
Or does this man genuinely believe his vile reputation in Okinomiya hasn't reached Hinamizawa...?
``A business trip...?@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1036. Where did you go...?``
``Haaah?@k@vS14/21/212000015. That ain't none of yer business...``
``...Mii... That's news to me.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1038. I didn't know that lounging around at some woman's house counted as a business trip.``
``Whaaaaaaat?!@k@vS14/21/212000017. Whaddya think yer sayin', ya little brat?!``
``Hehe, hehehe...!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1040. Ahahahaha, ahh ha ha ha ha ha!!``
I couldn't help it. It was so utterly ridiculous, the man in front of me looked so foolish, that I burst into wild laughter.

His lines were so predictable, like they'd been stamped from a template. I'd been wondering if he might say something different this time, but it was the same words, the same attitude. The consistency was so remarkable that I felt more pity than exasperation.
``Why youuuu!@k@vS14/21/212000019. I'll smash yer face in if ya keep mockin' me!!``
Completely enraged, Teppei forgot he was dealing with a child and grabbed me by the collar, lifting me up.
The lace at my neckline tore from the force, but as I stared directly into the face of the hideous beast before me, my heart was surprisingly calm.
...Had this come just a little earlier, I would have judged my current response as the worst possible choice.
If I quietly backed down here, Keiichi would surely save Satoko.
With his natural drive and strong leadership, he would rally friends, classmates, and even the Sonozaki family and the rest of the villagers, appealing to the child consultation center in Okinomiya.
...And though Satoko would suffer for a time, the passionate trust Keiichi and the others showed her would help her find the courage she'd forgotten, and she would break free from Teppei.
That was a miracle that had actually happened in a previous world.
And in this world too, there was a good chance the current Keiichi could make it happen again.
So at first, I had planned to wait for that...
``...R-Rika...!@k@vS14/04/210400259. P-please stop, Uncle!@k@vS14/04/210400260. Rika has nothing to do with this!!``
``Shut it!!@k@vS14/21/212000021. Go heat up the bath already!!``
``Rikaaa, run...!@k@vS14/04/210400262. Please, hurry and get away!!``
Satoko clung desperately to Teppei's legs, tears streaming down her face as she begged for mercy.
Looking down at her like that... I truly felt, deep in my heart, how grateful I was for Satoko's kindness, and how much it had saved me all this time.
...Satoko.
She still has that fever from earlier, doesn't she?
Yet she was dragged away by force and made to do housework...
And despite all that, she's shielding me without a thought for herself.
She has to know what kind of punishment awaits her for protecting me like this...
That's why...
``You little pest!@k@vS14/21/212000023. You defyin' me, huuuh?!``
That's why I... will fight!!
``Gwaaaaahh?!``
Teppei clutched at his eyes and shoved me away violently. Then he collapsed to the ground, writhing and wailing, his bright red face drenched in filthy tears and snot.
I charged at Teppei again, grabbed him by his greasy hair, and sprayed the pepper spray directly into his face.

Teppei desperately tried to turn away and shut his eyes, but the spray entered through his nose as well, irritating his mucous membranes. The combination of tears, snot, sneezing, and pain was so overwhelming he couldn't even lift his head.
``Gwaaaaahh...! Urgh...!@k@vS14/21/212000026. My eyes... gwaaaaahh... guh!!``
He rolled around on the ground desperately, flailing wildly in an attempt to keep me away.
...But compared to my fight with Shion long, long ago, his desperate thrashing was mere child's play.
``...Haaaaaahhh!!``
``Gwaaaaaahh!!``
I timed it carefully--the moment Teppei rolled and his face turned upward, I kicked with all my might.
I knew a kick from my thin legs, from a little girl's strength, wouldn't do much damage on its own... but hitting a vital spot changed everything!!
By sheer luck, the top of my left foot connected squarely with the bridge of Teppei's nose. He flipped over and crumpled to the ground, trembling.
``Let's go, Satoko!``
Satoko stared in a daze at my outstretched hand.
...She was stunned not only by my counterattack, but also by the sight of Teppei--her source of terror--reduced to this pathetic state, his face drenched from pepper spray, writhing in agony.
``Hurry, now!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1044. While Teppei can't move!``
``...Rika... I'm grateful for how you feel... truly.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1009b. But--``

``...You probably think that enduring this is atonement toward Satoshi, and that it makes you stronger.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1046. But that won't make anyone happy!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1047. It won't save anyone!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1048. Not even Satoshi!!``
``What does all that endurance and patience produce?@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1050. Do you think Satoshi disappeared because you didn't try hard enough before... So now you think you have to endure it?``
``That's wrong! You're mistaken! What Satoshi worked so hard for wasn't about enduring things!``
``He kept fighting bravely, throwing himself into the fray, because he wanted to protect you!!``
``Didn't you see it on your way here?@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1054. The people you thought were treating you coldly all this time--they stood up and tried to protect you from Teppei!``
``You felt that it would only burden them, so you gave in and went with this man without a fight!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1056. ...But troubling people and asking for help is never a bad thing!``
``What's truly wrong is turning your back on the feelings and the efforts of the people who risked themselves fighting for you!``
``Remember, Satoko!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1059. You must remember!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1060. Your brother, who stood alone against the adults to protect you!!``
``And another courageous brother, who gathered strength from nothing and fought to save you!!!``
``Even after all that, are you still going to keep ignoring the people who never gave up on you, who kept thinking of you until the very end, Satoko Houjou?!``
``Listen, Satoko, this is all about to be over!!@k@vS14/01/B_KEI082303. If you just say the word, even the mayor of Shishibone City will come to your aid!!``
``Heh heh, pretty amazing, right? The Sonozaki family arranged everything.@k@vS14/01/B_180101629. Even Mion's grandma stepped in to help!!``
For Satoko, the Sonozaki family had always been something she dreaded--people who openly badmouthed her.
Oryou was the one she hated most of all.
She couldn't comprehend why Oryou would go out of her way to help someone like her.
``...It's true that after the dam war, there were people in Hinamizawa who ignored and mistreated Satoko just for being a Houjou.``
``...I was too dense to notice, but Satoko, you've been living every day hurt by those cold stares.``
``But we smashed through all of that!!@k@vS14/01/B_KEI082304. There's not a single person left in Hinamizawa who would abandon you!!``
changeReason: Old uses singular 'I took care of' and 'treat you badly'; new correctly uses 俺たち (we) as 'we smashed through' and translates 見捨てる (abandon) as 'would abandon you' rather than the vaguer 'treat you badly.'

TextENGNew: ``I wish I could show you--right now, over two hundred people from Hinamizawa have gathered to support us!``
``They dropped everything--even the preparations for tomorrow's Watanagashi--and came to the consultation center to petition on your behalf!!``
Timidly, still with hesitation lingering in her heart.
Even so, Satoko listened to my words, and slowly began walking toward me.
Just a little more.
Just a little more, and I could reach her.
Unable to contain my anticipation, I took a step forward and stretched my hand out even further. Satoko responded by raising hers--
``...Whoa there?@k@vS14/00/chinA10002. Where d'ya think you're goin', Satoko-chaaaan~?``
I gasped and my entire body froze. Right before my eyes, Satoko had been seized from behind by a log-like arm, and she went rigid with shock.
``Ohhh, whatcha think yer doin', little girl~?@k@vS14/00/chinB10002. Wanna come play with us?@k@vS14/00/chinB10003. Hmmm?``
``Layin' hands on Tecchan, huh? That's Oyashiro-sama's little messenger for ya!``
One by one.
Three burly, rough-looking thugs emerged from the front door, leering at me with vulgar grins.
One of them had Satoko in a tight grip and wouldn't let go, while the other two cracked their knuckles with exaggerated menace as they closed in around me.
No way... He'd already called his cronies to the house...?!
But thinking about it for even a moment, it should have been obvious.

He was already a troublemaker in the village, and considering the scene he'd caused while dragging Satoko away from the shopping district, I should have expected him to call over fellow lowlifes for protection...!
...My failure was not thinking that far ahead. I bitterly cursed myself for losing my head.
If only... I had waited for Keiichi and the others before acting...
``R-Rikaaa, watch ooout!!``
``Take thiiiiis!!``
A kick struck me from behind without warning. It was all I could do to shield my face.
The full force of the blow connected with my side, sending me flying. I slammed into the ground, and a shock of pain so fierce it nearly knocked me unconscious coursed through my entire body.
``...You little braaaat...!@k@vS14/21/212000030. You seriously think you can mess with meeee?!``
Damn it... This man doesn't hold back even against a child...?!
Is it beyond common sense, or has no one ever taught him basic morality?
...Silently cursing them in my heart, I struggled to endure the searing pain tearing through my body and the ringing headache pounding in my skull as I stood up on trembling legs.
``R-Rikaaaaaaa!!@k@vS14/04/210400273. Stop it! Please, just stooooop!!``
``Aw man~, once Tecchan snaps there's no stopping him~!``
``But is this really okay?@k@vS14/00/chinB10005. She's still the village's guardian deity. If something happens, there'll be trouble.``
``Well, we'll step in before it goes too far.``
``Rikaaaa!!@k@vS14/04/210400275. Run! Hurry up and ruuun!!``
I could vaguely hear Satoko's tearful cries behind the men's vulgar conversation. Though even that was fading--my consciousness was growing hazy, and I could pass out at any moment.
Satoko... I'm sorry...

Only after experiencing this firsthand did I finally understand.@kI should have known how dangerous her situation was--I understood it intellectually.@k...And yet I had tried to bide my time, telling myself I'd wait for the right moment.
It might have already been too late.
Even though I'd said with my own mouth that there would be no more resets this time if I had regrets...!
How pathetic. Truly pathetic.@kI had simply been looking away from the worst-case scenario, never truly confronting it.@kTo think I couldn't understand that until I experienced it myself...!
``Huuuh?@k@vS14/21/212000032. What's that, you still want more?!@k@vS14/21/212000033. D'ya really wanna get killed, huh?!``
``Please, Uncle, Uncle!@k@vS14/04/210400277. Please, forgive Rika!!@k@vS14/04/210400278. I'll stay right here, I'll--!!``
``Don't say it, Satokoooo!!!``
Hearing my desperate, blood-curdling scream, Satoko turned her tear-soaked face toward me.
And I slowly shook my head, trying my best to form a reassuring smile.
``R-Rikaaaaa...!``
``...For the longest time... I had given up, believing I couldn't save you.``
``...But now, things are different...!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1068. With a strong enough will, fate doesn't matter--I'll break through any dead end!``
``...And action born from that will--that's what opens every door!!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1070. I'll prove it to you right here, right now...! I will!!``
Yes.
At last, I understood.
It's not willpower alone that pulls us toward the best possible fate.
It's the effort to act on those strong feelings--the struggle to keep moving.
So I won't run anymore. I won't give up.
I'm going to save Satoko.
And to do that--I won't let some worthless thug like this destroy our happiness!!
``...I won't fall...!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1072. ...I swear... I'm taking Satoko home!!``
``Wh-wha'd ya just saaaay?!!``
I... won't run anymore.
So please, lend me your strength... Hanyuu...!
God......... Keiichiii!!
``Whoooaaaaaaa--!!``
``--Gwaaah!!``
CRASH!!!
Something came hurtling in from behind Teppei at tremendous speed, aimed straight at him. Completely blindsided, Teppei couldn't dodge and took the full impact, sending him flying!
The object that had rammed into Teppei... was a bicycle. And riding it was--!
``Sorry I'm late, Rika-chan!!``
``K... Keiichiiii!!``

Overcome with joy, I tried to leap toward him, but my legs wouldn't cooperate and I fell into Keiichi's chest instead. And then all the pain and fear I'd been holding back burst forth in a flood of tears.
``You're late...!@k@vS14/05/210500823. You should learn to read the room a little better...!@k@vS14/05/210500824. If I'd gotten seriously hurt, what would you have done...?``
``...I'm sorry, I'm really sorry.@k@vS14/01/210101426. But it's all right now!@k@vS14/01/210101427. We'll protect Satoko!``
Then, still holding me against his chest, Keiichi turned to face the three thugs who had Satoko.
``...I'll keep this short.@k@vS14/01/210101429. Let Satoko go.@k@vS14/01/210101430. Unless you'd rather see hell.``
``Huuuuh?@k@vS14/00/chinA10005. You got some nerve, you little punk!@k@vS14/00/chinA10006. Ya got a deathwish or somethin', huh?!``
``Whaddya think one kid can do against us, huh?!``
``We'll break every bone in your body! Brace yourself!!``
``Hah!@k@vS14/01/210101432. Don't talk big, you small fry!@k@vS14/01/210101433. You can't even talk tough without hiding behind a little girl, you cowards!!``
``Ganging up is standard practice for lowlifes, but needing a hostage just to pick a fight? You're truly the lowest of the low!!``
``Whaa?!@k@vS14/00/chinC10005. Whadja just saaaay?!``

Their attempt at intimidation had backfired spectacularly, and the three thugs kept shouting to maintain their bravado. Then, as if to assert their numerical advantage, one of them lunged at Keiichi with a punch!
``Oh yeeeaah?!@k@vS14/00/chinB10008. You're deaaaaaad!!``
``Whaaa?!``
``Too slow, too slow, too slow, too slow!!@k@vS14/01/210101437. Compared to Rena's punches, your third-rate attacks look like slow motion! Pathetic!!``
``Gwah!!``
Keiichi slipped past the howling thug's log-like arm by a hair's breadth, then caught him off guard with an elbow strike buried deep in his side.
The perfectly placed blow carried his full body weight behind it. The man screamed, became tangled up with Keiichi, and crashed to the ground.
``You bastaaaard!!@k@vS14/00/chinC10007. Keep disrespectin' us and I'll kill yooooou!!``
``Y-you son of a bitch...!!``
``Cocky little punk...!@k@vS14/21/212000038. No more holding baaack!!``
The man Keiichi had struck, along with Teppei who had recovered, all surrounded Keiichi--three against one, a cowardly move.
...This is bad...! With the size difference, and attacks coming from every direction, even Keiichi can't possibly hold his own...!
``...Damn it...!@k@vS14/05/210500827. Keiichi... Keiichiii...!!``
I tried to go help him, but my legs had no strength left, and I couldn't even approach.
At times like these, I truly resented this small, helpless body of mine.
Before long, a blow he couldn't block connected with Keiichi's solar plexus, and he doubled over in pain.
Then a kick struck his back from behind, and Keiichi finally dropped to his knees and crumpled.
``...S-stop it...!@k@vS14/04/210400281. Please stop! Keiichi-san, run awaaay!!``
Seeing Keiichi at such a dire disadvantage, Satoko wailed and stretched her hand toward him. But held fast in a headlock by the remaining thug, all she could do was thrash about helplessly.
``Keiichi-san, Rika, that's enough! Please, that's enough!!@k@vS14/04/210400283. Just leave me and run!!``
``No more... I can't take it...!!@k@vS14/04/210400285. Please, don't hurt them anymore!!@k@vS14/04/210400286. I'll stay, I'll stay right--!!``
``Believe in us, Satokooooo!!!``
Just as Satoko, unable to bear watching any longer, was about to utter words of surrender, Keiichi whipped his head up, eyes blazing, and shouted at her with all his might.
His voice--brimming with unyielding spirit and conviction even in this desperate situation--made Satoko catch her breath and look at him.
``Satoko... listen, we decided to save you!!@k@vS14/01/210101445. This isn't someone else's will, or fate, or because you asked us for help!!``
``I refuse to sit back and watch you get hurt, and I refuse to comfort myself by saying 'someday we'll save her'!!@k@vS14/01/210101447. We chose this ourselves--to save you, right here, right now!``
``K-Keiichi-san...!``
``...Satoko!@k@vS14/01/210101449. No matter how long you endure, God is too busy to send miracles your way!``
``And even if I spot someone drowning... my arms are only so long. If you don't reach out to meet me, I can't grab you!!@k@vS14/01/210101451. So don't give up--reach your hand out from there!!``
``I won't give up! My resolve to save you will never, ever waver!!``
``So Satoko, summon your courage and show us the strength of your will!!@k@vS14/01/210101454. Answering each other's trust is what being friends is about--and we are friends!``
``......Keiichi-san... Keiichi-san...!!!``
``Gwaaahh?!``
``...Satoko?!``
At last... yes, at long last, Satoko seemed to reach a decision. She lifted her head with fierce determination and sank her teeth into the arm of the man holding her.
When he loosened his grip from the pain, she tore free, charged straight at Teppei who had Keiichi surrounded, and sent him flying with a full-body tackle!!
``...I won't lose...!!``

Satoko quickly sprang to her feet and squared off against Teppei, who roared in outrage at the unexpected counterattack. The light that had been fading from her eyes only moments ago had returned, blazing with fierce intensity.
``I won't lose to you...!@k@vS14/04/210400292. I will not lose to the likes of you!!@k@vS14/04/210400293. I despise you! I absolutely refuse to lose to you!!``
``Whaaaaaa?!``
Driven by rage, Teppei swung at Satoko without mercy.
But Satoko used her small frame and quick reflexes as weapons, dodging every single one of his blows.
Then, as Teppei lost his composure, she swiftly ducked low and caught the moment his weight shifted off his right foot, tackling his leg out from under him.
``Gaahh!!``
Caught off guard, Teppei hit the ground. But Satoko was also thrown off by the impact and tumbled across the ground, landing on her backside.
``Haah, haah...!@k@vS14/04/210400296. H-how pathetic, you out-of-shape middle-aged oaf...!!``
``...Wha'd you saaaay?!``
With a bellowing roar, Teppei raised his fist and swung at her.

...Just moments ago, that alone would have been enough to make Satoko freeze in terror. But now she dodged nimbly. And the instant she noticed his weight shift backward off his supporting leg, she hooked his foot and swept it out from under him.
Teppei stumbled forward and splattered face-first onto the ground, with Satoko looking down at him. The tables had completely turned.
Teppei got up yet again and threw punch after punch, kick after kick at Satoko, screaming with murderous fury.
But in the end, he was nothing but a petty bully who only knew how to lord it over the weak! His brute-force attacks meant absolutely nothing against Satoko!
She quickly read his reach, and the angrier he got, the more his strikes lost their accuracy.
And as they faced each other, Satoko began to feel almost amused that she had ever been afraid of a man like this...
``Oh ho ho ho...!@k@vS14/04/210400298. They say the weaker the dog, the louder it barks. And you're a prime example, Uncle!``
``Shut yer trap!!@k@vS14/21/212000046. ...You ungrateful brat, you murdered your own parents!!``
The moment she heard those words, Satoko froze as if turned to ice.
No...! That's the one thing he must never be allowed to say...!!
``Who d'ya think yer talkin' to, ya little shit?!@k@vS14/21/212000048. You pushed your own parents into the river for the insurance money, didn't ya?!``
``Stop iiiiiiit!!!``
Summoning every last shred of strength, I threw myself at Teppei. But my feeble attack couldn't budge him--instead I was knocked back and tumbled to the ground beside Satoko.

When I managed to lift myself up, Satoko was crouched behind me, trembling violently, clutching her head. I crawled over to her desperately and pulled her into an embrace, fighting through my own throbbing pain.
``S-Satoko...!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1075. Don't! Don't think about it!!@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1076. It's all lies--...?!``
I looked up with a start and there was Teppei, looming over us with a hideous expression on his face.
``...Just die alreaaaaady!!``
``That's enough, Teppei Houjou!!``
Just then.
For a moment, I thought the voice was a hallucination.
It couldn't be... There was no way. To hope for something like that was completely unreasonable.
Because...
Because there was no way he would come to a place like this...?!
``Satokooo!!``

I heard Shion's voice, and sensed her rushing over to embrace Satoko beside me. But more than that, I needed to confirm the voice that still lingered in my ears. Slowly, fearfully... I raised my face.
``...Rika-chan!!``
And there, beyond where I'd turned to look--he was running toward me.
No way...
Can I really... believe this...?
Don't you dare call this a dream...!
And then, as if to shelter me, he wrapped me in his broad arms and held me against his chest.
``A... Akasaka...?``
``I came to help...@k@vS14/13/211300087. Rika-chan, are you all right?``
``Akasaka... Akasakaaaaaa!!!``
I had wanted to see you for so long.
You were the first person I ever wished would come save me.
Finally, here...
To think you'd finally come, at a time like this...!!
``...Sorry I'm late.@k@vS14/13/211300089. It's all right now. You can relax...``
``Akasaka... uuuu, Akasakaaa...!!``
Overwhelmed with emotion, I cried out and threw myself into Akasaka's chest, sobbing uncontrollably.
He held me protectively as he rose to his feet, then spoke in a clear, commanding voice to Teppei, whose face was contorted with fear and cold sweat.
``Teppei Houjou, and his accomplices.@k@vS14/13/211300091. ...This is the police.@k@vS14/13/211300092. You are under arrest on suspicion of violating the Narcotics Control Act, as well as assault!!``
On that signal, police officers who had been surrounding the area converged all at once to apprehend the men.
Realizing they were cornered, the thugs scattered in a panic, their faces twisted with shock and fear, desperately scrambling toward the woods out back.
``Halt!!``
``Don't let them get away!!``

A number of officers gave chase, and before long several screams echoed through the area. Perhaps relieved by the sight, the pain suddenly rushed back through my entire body, and I collapsed where I stood.
``Rika-chan, are you all right?!``
``...I'm fine.@k@vS14/05/210500839. More importantly, shouldn't you be going after Teppei?``
``That's Ooishi-san's job.@k@vS14/13/211300095. ...Isn't that right?``
``Nfu fu fu!@k@vS14/11/210700092. Akasaka-san, please don't steal my lines.``

``A member of the Metropolitan Police has no authority to arrest local criminals, do they?@k@vS14/11/210700094. And citing the Narcotics Control Act without a warrant--you shouldn't be telling fibs~!``
I stared at Akasaka in bewilderment, and he looked back with a sheepish, wry smile. ...So that was... a bluff?
``...If policemen are telling lies, Japan's police force is finished.``
``Ahahaha.@k@vS14/13/211300097. ...But the way they reacted, they're probably not entirely innocent after all.``
``...Ooishi-san, let's search the house just to be safe.``
``Like I said, we can't do that without a warrant.@k@vS14/11/210700096. Are you really a government official?``
``You can make the paperwork work out later, can't you?@k@vS14/13/211300100. ...Am I wrong?``
``Nah ha ha ha!@k@vS14/11/210700098. You've gotten awfully crafty since we last met~!``
``...Kuma-chan, let's go ahead and send some people inside to take a look.``
``Roger!!``
The detective called Kuma-chan nodded and led three others into the house.
Near the entrance, Keiichi sat slumped on the ground, utterly spent, while Shion held Satoko tightly, tears of relief streaming down her face.
``Are you all right, Keiichi...?``
``...Yeah!@k@vS14/01/210101456. This is nothing!``
Keiichi wiped the blood from his lip with his fist and flashed a grin--he must have cut the inside of his mouth. Seeing that strong, forced bravery meant to reassure me, I was finally able to smile.
But--that moment of relief lasted only an instant.
``...Satoko?@k@vS14/06/210600657. What's wrong, Satoko?!``

Shion's urgent, panicked cry rang out and snapped me back to reality. When I looked, Satoko was in Shion's arms, clutching her head, trembling violently, muttering deliriously as she shook her head over and over, her eyes hollow and vacant.
``...Th-that's not true...!@k@vS14/04/210400303. It wasn't me...!@k@vS14/04/210400304. I didn't... I didn't do anything...!!``
``Satoko!!``
``Satoko, hang in there... Satoko!!``
